title: Σ₀ Bitcoin-puzzle structure test — the asymmetric oracle

synthetic_ground_truth: independent_uniform_keys(40): per-puzzle LCG draws, no generator
not_claimed: no verdict on real keys is asserted unless the TSV is present

ground_truth_mechanism.creator_statement: There is no pattern. It is just consecutive keys from a deterministic wallet (masked with leading 000...0001 to set difficulty). It is simply a crude measuring instrument, of the cracking strength of the community.
ground_truth_mechanism.interpretation: Keys are independent one-way HD-wallet derivations, each masked to N bits to set the range. One-way derivation ⇒ no relation between solved and unsolved keys ⇒ no findable generator. This EXPLAINS the empirical UNDECIDED: a generator exists (wallet seed) but is cryptographically unfindable, never provably random (Chaitin).

convergence_record.hypothesis: A short generator behind the solved puzzle keys could predict the next unsolved key (an edge over the pool).
convergence_record.evidence: Instrument validated on ground truth (correct=True): a real nested generator yields interior prediction 39/39 EXACT yet next-key predictable=False; structureless draws yield verdict UNDECIDED.
convergence_record.result: CLOSED by two convergent sources: (1) the instrument returns UNDECIDED on 82 verified real keys (nesting falsified ~2/69, chance-level); (2) the creator states 'There is no pattern... consecutive keys from a deterministic wallet.' A generator exists (HD seed) but is one-way and unfindable; never provably random. No structural edge — the only route is brute-force search (a negative-EV lottery). My nested-master-key prediction was FALSIFIED.
